free_text: Elastic Scattering of 50 MeV Protons by Nuclei in the
Range from Ca-42 to Pb-208.
Last checking has been done.
DATE is corrected
The Targets were All 2 Mg/Cm**2 Thick with an Error of
Around 5%. They were All Isotopically Enriched to
Around 90-99%. The Isotopic Impurities were Known and
The Absolute Cross Section was Corrected For these by
An Iterative Procedure.
For Polarization Measurements Above 30 Degree, Oxide
Targets of Thickness 5-10 Mg/Cm**2 were Used.  The
Oxygen in the Targets at the Angles at Which They were
Used did not Interfere with the Elastic Scattering
Measurements.
.Optical Model Calculations.
.The Focal Plane of the Magnetic Spectrometer
Was 45.7-Cm Long.  An Acoustic Spark Chamber Placed
Along the Focal Plane Measured the Position of the
Particles.
Two Thin Plastic Scintillators.  The
Scintillators were 254-Mum Thick.
*by Authors* the Correction Due to Neutron Coincidence.
This Correction was Less Than 3% at All Angles.
The Proton Linear Accelerator (PLA) At
Rutherford High Energy Laboratory.
Maximum Polarization of 55%.
The Mean Beam Current at the End of the
Accelerator was of the Order of 2 Mua.
The Error in the Absolute Cross Section Mainly
Arose from the Target Thickness, Counting Statistics
And Charge Measurements. The Errors were in General of
The Order of 5-7%.  Errors in the Polarization
Measurements were Mainly Due to Statistics and were
Between 5-15%.
.Data-Point Reader Uncertainty.
.By CAJAD.
